186.name: Low Kick
186.description: A powerful low kick that makes the target fall over. The heavier the target, the greater the move's power.

186.name: Natural Gift
186.description: The user draws power to attack by using its held Berry. The Berry determines its type and power.

186.name: Return
186.description: An attack that is based on loyalty.

186.name: Seismic Toss
186.description: The target is thrown using the power of gravity. It inflicts damage equal to the user's level.

186.name: Amnesia
186.description: The user temporarily empties its mind to forget its concerns. This sharply boosts the user's Sp. Def stat.

186.name: Attract
186.description: If the target is of the opposite gender to the user, it becomes infatuated with the user and will sometimes be unable to use its moves.

186.name: Belly Drum
186.description: The user maximizes its Attack stat but loses HP equal to half its max HP.

186.name: Captivate
186.description: If it is the opposite gender of the user, the foe is charmed into sharply lowering its Sp. Atk stat.

186.name: Confide
186.description: The user tells the target a secret, and the target loses its ability to concentrate. This lowers the target's Sp. Atk stat.

186.name: Curse
186.description: A move that has different effects depending on whether the user is a Ghost type or not.

186.name: Defense Curl
186.description: The user curls up and boosts its Defense stat.

186.name: Detect
186.description: This move enables the user to protect itself from all attacks. Its chance of failing rises if it is used in succession.

186.name: Double Team
186.description: By moving rapidly, the user makes illusory copies of itself to boost its evasiveness.

186.name: Encore
186.description: The user compels the target to keep using the move it encored for three turns.

186.name: Endure
186.description: The user endures any attack with at least 1 HP. This move's chance of failing rises if used in succession.

186.name: Hail
186.description: Summons a hailstorm that strikes every turn.

186.name: Haze
186.description: The user creates a haze that eliminates every stat change among all the Pokémon engaged in battle.

186.name: Helping Hand
186.description: The user assists an ally by boosting the power of that ally's attack.

186.name: Hypnosis
186.description: The user employs hypnotic suggestion to make the target fall asleep.

186.name: Metronome
186.description: The user waggles a finger and stimulates its brain into randomly using nearly any move.

186.name: Mimic
186.description: The user copies the move last used by the target. The copied move can be used until the user of Mimic leaves the battle.

186.name: Mist
186.description: The user cloaks itself and its allies in a white mist that prevents any of their stats from being lowered for five turns.

186.name: Perish Song
186.description: Any Pokémon that hears this song faints in three turns, unless it switches out of battle.

186.name: Protect
186.description: This move enables the user to protect itself from all attacks. Its chance of failing rises if it is used in succession.

186.name: Psych Up
186.description: The user hypnotizes itself into copying any stat change made by the target.

186.name: Rain Dance
186.description: The user summons a heavy rain that falls for five turns, powering up Water-type attacks. The rain also lowers the power of Fire-type attacks.

186.name: Rest
186.description: The user goes to sleep for two turns. This fully restores the user's HP and cures any status conditions.

186.name: Screech
186.description: An earsplitting screech harshly lowers the target's Defense stat.

186.name: Sleep Talk
186.description: The user randomly uses one of the moves it knows. This move can only be used while the user is asleep.

186.name: Splash
186.description: The user just flops and splashes around to no effect at all...

186.name: Substitute
186.description: The user creates a substitute for itself using some of its own HP. The substitute serves as the user's decoy.

186.name: Swagger
186.description: The user enrages and confuses the target. However, this also sharply boosts the target's Attack stat.

186.name: Toxic
186.description: A move that leaves the target badly poisoned. Its poison damage worsens every turn.

196.name: Giga Impact
196.description: The user charges at the target using every bit of its power. The user can't move on the next turn.

196.name: Hyper Beam
196.description: The target is attacked with a powerful beam. The user can't move on the next turn.

196.name: Last Resort
196.description: This move can be used only after the user has used all the other moves it knows in the battle.

196.name: Double-Edge
196.description: A reckless life-risking tackle in which the user rushes the target. This also damages the user quite a lot.

196.name: Future Sight
196.description: Two turns after this move is used, a hunk of psychic energy attacks the target.

196.name: Zap Cannon
196.description: The user attacks by firing an electric blast like a cannon. This also leaves the target with paralysis.

196.name: Dream Eater
196.description: The user eats the dreams of a sleeping target. The user's HP is restored by up to half the damage taken by the target.

196.name: Iron Tail
196.description: The target is slammed with a steel-hard tail. This may also lower the target's Defense stat.

196.name: Hyper Voice
196.description: The user attacks by letting loose a horribly loud, resounding cry.

196.name: Psychic
196.description: The target is hit with a strong telekinetic force to inflict damage. This may also lower the target’s Sp. Def stat.

196.name: Take Down
196.description: A reckless full-body charge attack for slamming into the target. This also damages the user a little.

196.name: Body Slam
196.description: The user attacks by dropping onto the target with its full body weight. This may also leave the target with paralysis.

196.name: Psychic Fangs
196.description: The user attacks by biting the target with its psychic capabilities. This move can also break barriers, such as Light Screen and Reflect.

196.name: Alluring Voice
196.description: The user attacks the target using its angelic voice. This also confuses the target if its stats have been boosted during the turn.

196.name: Dazzling Gleam
196.description: The user damages opposing Pokémon by emitting a powerful flash.

196.name: Dig
196.description: The user burrows into the ground on the first turn, then attacks on the next turn.

196.name: Expanding Force
196.description: The user attacks the target with its psychic power. When the ground is Psychic Terrain, this move's power is boosted and it damages all opposing Pokémon.

196.name: Power Gem
196.description: The user attacks with a ray of light that sparkles as if it were made of gemstones.

196.name: Psyshock
196.description: The user materializes an odd psychic wave to attack the target. This move deals physical damage.

196.name: Shadow Ball
196.description: The user attacks by hurling a shadowy blob at the target. This may also lower the target’s Sp. Def stat.

196.name: Tera Blast
196.description: If the user has Terastallized, it unleashes energy of its Tera Type. This move inflicts damage using the Attack or Sp. Atk stat—whichever is higher for the user.

196.name: Zen Headbutt
196.description: The user focuses its willpower to its head and attacks the target. This may also make the target flinch.

196.name: Psychic Noise
196.description: The user attacks the target with unpleasant sound waves. For two turns, the target is prevented from recovering HP through moves, Abilities, or held items.

196.name: Signal Beam
196.description: A strange beam attack that may confuse the foe.

196.name: Facade
196.description: This move's power is doubled if the user is poisoned, burned, or paralyzed.

196.name: Headbutt
196.description: The user sticks out its head and attacks by charging straight into the target. This may also make the target flinch.

196.name: Retaliate
196.description: The user takes revenge for a fainted ally. This move's power is boosted if an ally fainted in the previous turn.

196.name: Secret Power
196.description: An attack with effects that vary by location.

196.name: Psybeam
196.description: The target is attacked with a peculiar ray. This may also confuse the target.

196.name: Bite
196.description: The target is bitten with viciously sharp fangs. This may also make the target flinch.

196.name: Covet
196.description: The user endearingly approaches the target, then steals the target's held item.

196.name: Hidden Power
196.description: The power varies with the POKéMON.

196.name: Magical Leaf
196.description: The user scatters curious leaves that chase the target. This attack never misses.

196.name: Round
196.description: The user attacks the target with a song. If others use this move, they will act immediately after the initial user, and the power of their Rounds will be boosted.

196.name: Swift
196.description: Star-shaped rays are shot at opposing Pokémon. This attack never misses.

196.name: Confusion
196.description: The target is hit with a weak telekinetic force to inflict damage. This may also confuse the target.

196.name: Cut
196.description: Cuts using claws, scythes, etc.

196.name: Draining Kiss
196.description: The user steals the target's HP with a kiss. The user's HP is restored by over half the damage taken by the target.

196.name: Snore
196.description: This attack can be used only if the user is asleep. The harsh noise may also make the target flinch.

196.name: Trailblaze
196.description: The user attacks suddenly as if leaping out from tall grass. The user's nimble footwork boosts its Speed stat.

196.name: Weather Ball
196.description: This move's type and power change depending on the weather at the time the move is used.

196.name: Echoed Voice
196.description: The user attacks the target with an echoing voice. If this move is used consecutively by any Pokémon, its power is boosted.

196.name: Pay Day
196.description: Coins are hurled at the target to inflict damage. Money is earned after the battle.

196.name: Quick Attack
196.description: The user lunges at the target to inflict damage, moving at blinding speed. This move always goes first.

196.name: Tackle
196.description: A physical attack in which the user charges and slams into the target with its whole body.

196.name: Mud-Slap
196.description: The user hurls mud in the target's face to inflict damage and lower its accuracy.

196.name: Stored Power
196.description: The user attacks the target with stored power. The more the user's stats are boosted, the greater the move's power.

196.name: Frustration
196.description: An attack based on lack of loyalty.

196.name: Grass Knot
196.description: The user snares the target with grass and trips it. The heavier the target, the greater the move's power.

196.name: Natural Gift
196.description: The user draws power to attack by using its held Berry. The Berry determines its type and power.

196.name: Return
196.description: An attack that is based on loyalty.

196.name: Ally Switch
196.description: The user teleports using a strange power and switches places with one of its allies. This move's chance of failing rises if it is used in succession.

196.name: Attract
196.description: If the target is of the opposite gender to the user, it becomes infatuated with the user and will sometimes be unable to use its moves.

196.name: Baby-Doll Eyes
196.description: The user stares at the target with its baby-doll eyes, which lowers the target's Attack stat. This move always goes first.

196.name: Baton Pass
196.description: The user switches places with a party Pokémon in waiting and passes along any stat changes.

196.name: Calm Mind
196.description: The user quietly focuses its mind and calms its spirit to boost its Sp. Atk and Sp. Def stats.

196.name: Captivate
196.description: If it is the opposite gender of the user, the foe is charmed into sharply lowering its Sp. Atk stat.

196.name: Charm
196.description: The user gazes at the target rather charmingly, making it less wary. This harshly lowers the target's Attack stat.

196.name: Confide
196.description: The user tells the target a secret, and the target loses its ability to concentrate. This lowers the target's Sp. Atk stat.

196.name: Confuse Ray
196.description: The target is exposed to a sinister ray that causes confusion.

196.name: Copycat
196.description: The user mimics the move used immediately before it. The move fails if no other move has been used yet.

196.name: Curse
196.description: A move that has different effects depending on whether the user is a Ghost type or not.

196.name: Detect
196.description: This move enables the user to protect itself from all attacks. Its chance of failing rises if it is used in succession.

196.name: Double Team
196.description: By moving rapidly, the user makes illusory copies of itself to boost its evasiveness.

196.name: Endure
196.description: The user endures any attack with at least 1 HP. This move's chance of failing rises if used in succession.

196.name: Fake Tears
196.description: The user feigns crying to fluster the target. This harshly lowers the target's Sp. Def stat.

196.name: Flash
196.description: Blinds the foe to reduce accuracy.

196.name: Focus Energy
196.description: The user takes a deep breath and focuses so that its future attacks have a heightened chance of landing critical hits.

196.name: Gravity
196.description: Enables Flying types or Pokémon with the Levitate Ability to be hit by Ground-type moves for five turns. Moves that involve flying can't be used.

196.name: Growl
196.description: The user growls in an endearing way, making opposing Pokémon less wary. This lowers their Attack stats.

196.name: Heal Bell
196.description: The user makes a soothing bell chime to cure the status conditions of all its fellow party Pokémon and allies.

196.name: Helping Hand
196.description: The user assists an ally by boosting the power of that ally's attack.

196.name: Imprison
196.description: If opposing Pokémon know any move also known by the user, they are prevented from using it.

196.name: Laser Focus
196.description: The user concentrates intensely. The attack on the next turn always results in a critical hit.

196.name: Light Screen
196.description: A wondrous wall of light is put up to reduce damage from special moves for five turns.

196.name: Magic Coat
196.description: Reflects special effects back to the attacker.

196.name: Magic Room
196.description: The user creates a bizarre area in which Pokémon's held items lose their effects for five turns.

196.name: Mimic
196.description: The user copies the move last used by the target. The copied move can be used until the user of Mimic leaves the battle.

196.name: Morning Sun
196.description: The user restores its own HP. The amount of HP regained varies with the weather.

196.name: Nightmare
196.description: A sleeper loses 1/4 HP every turn.

196.name: Power Swap
196.description: The user employs its psychic power to switch changes to its Attack and Sp. Atk stats with the target.

196.name: Protect
196.description: This move enables the user to protect itself from all attacks. Its chance of failing rises if it is used in succession.

196.name: Psych Up
196.description: The user hypnotizes itself into copying any stat change made by the target.

196.name: Psychic Terrain
196.description: This protects Pokémon on the ground from priority moves and powers up Psychic-type moves for five turns.

196.name: Rain Dance
196.description: The user summons a heavy rain that falls for five turns, powering up Water-type attacks. The rain also lowers the power of Fire-type attacks.

196.name: Reflect
196.description: A wondrous wall of light is put up to reduce damage from physical moves for five turns.

196.name: Rest
196.description: The user goes to sleep for two turns. This fully restores the user's HP and cures any status conditions.

196.name: Roar
196.description: The target is scared off, and a different Pokémon is dragged out. In the wild, this ends a battle against a single Pokémon.

196.name: Sand Attack
196.description: Sand is hurled in the target's face, lowering the target's accuracy.

196.name: Skill Swap
196.description: The user employs its psychic power to exchange Abilities with the target.

196.name: Sleep Talk
196.description: The user randomly uses one of the moves it knows. This move can only be used while the user is asleep.

196.name: Substitute
196.description: The user creates a substitute for itself using some of its own HP. The substitute serves as the user's decoy.

196.name: Sunny Day
196.description: The user intensifies the sun for five turns, powering up Fire-type attacks. The sunlight also lowers the power of Water-type attacks.

196.name: Swagger
196.description: The user enrages and confuses the target. However, this also sharply boosts the target's Attack stat.

196.name: Tail Whip
196.description: The user wags its tail cutely, making opposing Pokémon less wary. This lowers their Defense stats.

196.name: Telekinesis
196.description: The user makes the target float with its psychic power. The target is easier to hit for three turns.

196.name: Thunder Wave
196.description: The user launches a weak jolt of electricity that paralyzes the target.

196.name: Toxic
196.description: A move that leaves the target badly poisoned. Its poison damage worsens every turn.

196.name: Trick
196.description: The user catches the target off guard and swaps the target's held item with its own.

196.name: Trick Room
196.description: The user creates a bizarre area in which slower Pokémon get to move first for five turns.

196.name: Work Up
196.description: The user is roused, and its Attack and Sp. Atk stats are boosted.

214.name: Upper Hand
214.description: The user reacts to the target's movement and strikes with the heel of its palm, making the target flinch. This move fails if the target is not readying a priority move.

214.name: Venoshock
214.description: The user drenches the target in a special poisonous liquid. This move's power is doubled if the target is poisoned.

214.name: Aerial Ace
214.description: The user confounds the target with speed, then slashes. This attack never misses.

214.name: Assurance
214.description: This move's power is doubled if the target has already taken some damage in the same turn.

214.name: Brutal Swing
214.description: The user swings its body around violently to inflict damage on everything in its vicinity.

214.name: Bug Bite
214.description: The user attacks by biting the target. If the target is holding a Berry, the user eats it and gains its effect.

214.name: Bulldoze
214.description: The user strikes everything around it by stomping down on the ground. This lowers the Speed stats of those hit.

214.name: Hidden Power
214.description: The power varies with the POKéMON.

214.name: Revenge
214.description: An attack that gains power if injured by the foe.

214.name: Rock Tomb
214.description: The user hurls boulders at the target to inflict damage. This also lowers the target's Speed stat by cutting off its movement.

214.name: Round
214.description: The user attacks the target with a song. If others use this move, they will act immediately after the initial user, and the power of their Rounds will be boosted.

214.name: Thief
214.description: The user attacks and steals the target's held item simultaneously. The user can't steal anything if it already holds an item.

214.name: Cut
214.description: Cuts using claws, scythes, etc.

214.name: Pounce
214.description: The user attacks by pouncing on the target. This also lowers the target's Speed stat.

214.name: Smack Down
214.description: The user throws a stone or similar projectile to attack the target. If the target is in the air, it will be knocked down to the ground.

214.name: Snore
214.description: This attack can be used only if the user is asleep. The harsh noise may also make the target flinch.

214.name: Struggle Bug
214.description: The user attacks by struggling against opposing Pokémon. This also lowers their Sp. Atk stats.

214.name: Trailblaze
214.description: The user attacks suddenly as if leaping out from tall grass. The user's nimble footwork boosts its Speed stat.

214.name: False Swipe
214.description: A restrained attack that prevents the target from fainting. The target is left with at least 1 HP.

214.name: Fury Cutter
214.description: The user attacks by slashing the target with scythes, claws, or the like. This attack becomes more powerful if it hits in succession.

214.name: Pursuit
214.description: Heavily strikes switching POKéMON.

214.name: Rock Smash
214.description: The user attacks with a punch. This may also lower the target's Defense stat.

214.name: Tackle
214.description: A physical attack in which the user charges and slams into the target with its whole body.

214.description: The user whirls its fists to send a wave of pure vacuum at the target. This move always goes first.

214.name: Rock Blast
214.description: The user attacks by hurling hard rocks at the target. This move hits two to five times in a row.

214.name: Arm Thrust
214.description: The user attacks the target with open-palmed arm thrusts. This move hits two to five times in a row.

214.name: Fury Attack
214.description: The user attacks by jabbing the target with a horn, a beak, or the like. This move hits two to five times in a row.

214.name: Bide

214.name: Counter
214.description: A retaliatory attack that counters any physical move, inflicting double the damage taken.

214.name: Endeavor
214.description: The user inflicts damage by cutting down the target's HP to roughly equal the user's HP.

214.name: Flail
214.description: The user flails about aimlessly to attack. The less HP the user has, the greater the move's power.

214.description: The user flings its held item at the target to attack. This move's power and effects depend on the item.

214.name: Frustration
214.description: An attack based on lack of loyalty.

214.description: A powerful low kick that makes the target fall over. The heavier the target, the greater the move's power.

214.description: The user draws power to attack by using its held Berry. The Berry determines its type and power.

214.name: Return
214.description: An attack that is based on loyalty.

214.name: Reversal
214.description: An all-out attack that becomes more powerful the less HP the user has.

214.name: Seismic Toss
214.description: The target is thrown using the power of gravity. It inflicts damage equal to the user's level.

214.name: Attract
214.description: If the target is of the opposite gender to the user, it becomes infatuated with the user and will sometimes be unable to use its moves.

214.name: Bulk Up
214.description: The user tenses its muscles to bulk up its body, boosting its Attack and Defense stats.

214.name: Captivate
214.description: If it is the opposite gender of the user, the foe is charmed into sharply lowering its Sp. Atk stat.

214.name: Coaching
214.description: The user properly coaches its ally Pokémon, boosting their Attack and Defense stats.

214.name: Confide
214.description: The user tells the target a secret, and the target loses its ability to concentrate. This lowers the target's Sp. Atk stat.

214.name: Curse
214.description: A move that has different effects depending on whether the user is a Ghost type or not.

214.name: Detect
214.description: This move enables the user to protect itself from all attacks. Its chance of failing rises if it is used in succession.

214.name: Double Team
214.description: By moving rapidly, the user makes illusory copies of itself to boost its evasiveness.

214.name: Endure
214.description: The user endures any attack with at least 1 HP. This move's chance of failing rises if used in succession.

214.name: Harden
214.description: The user stiffens all the muscles in its body to boost its Defense stat.

214.name: Helping Hand

214.name: Iron Defense
214.description: The user hardens its body's surface like iron, sharply boosting its Defense stat.

214.name: Leer
214.description: The user gives opposing Pokémon an intimidating leer that lowers their Defense stats.

214.name: Mimic
214.description: The user copies the move last used by the target. The copied move can be used until the user of Mimic leaves the battle.

214.name: Protect
214.description: This move enables the user to protect itself from all attacks. Its chance of failing rises if it is used in succession.

214.name: Rain Dance
214.description: The user summons a heavy rain that falls for five turns, powering up Water-type attacks. The rain also lowers the power of Fire-type attacks.

214.name: Rest

214.name: Sleep Talk
214.description: The user randomly uses one of the moves it knows. This move can only be used while the user is asleep.

214.name: Spikes
214.description: The user lays a trap of spikes at the feet of the opposing team. The spikes will damage opposing Pokémon that switch into battle.

214.name: Substitute
214.description: The user creates a substitute for itself using some of its own HP. The substitute serves as the user's decoy.

214.name: Sunny Day
214.description: The user intensifies the sun for five turns, powering up Fire-type attacks. The sunlight also lowers the power of Water-type attacks.

214.name: Swagger
214.description: The user enrages and confuses the target. However, this also sharply boosts the target's Attack stat.

214.name: Swords Dance

214.name: Toxic

214.name: Work Up

229.description: This move enables the user to protect itself from all attacks. Its chance of failing rises if it is used in succession.

229.name: Double Team
229.description: By moving rapidly, the user makes illusory copies of itself to boost its evasiveness.

229.name: Embargo
229.description: It prevents the foe from using its held item. Its Trainer is also prevented from using items on it.

229.description: The user endures any attack with at least 1 HP. This move's chance of failing rises if used in succession.

229.name: Helping Hand

229.description: The user howls loudly to rouse itself and its allies. This boosts their Attack stats.

229.name: Laser Focus
229.description: The user concentrates intensely. The attack on the next turn always results in a critical hit.

229.name: Leer
229.description: The user gives opposing Pokémon an intimidating leer that lowers their Defense stats.

229.name: Mimic
229.description: The user copies the move last used by the target. The copied move can be used until the user of Mimic leaves the battle.

229.name: Nasty Plot
229.description: The user stimulates its brain by thinking bad thoughts. This sharply boosts the user's Sp. Atk stat.

229.name: Nightmare
229.description: A sleeper loses 1/4 HP every turn.

229.name: Odor Sleuth
229.description: Negates the foe’s efforts to heighten evasiveness.

229.name: Pain Split
229.description: The user adds its HP to the target's HP, then equally splits the combined HP between the target and itself.

229.name: Protect
229.description: This move enables the user to protect itself from all attacks. Its chance of failing rises if it is used in succession.

229.name: Rain Dance
229.description: The user summons a heavy rain that falls for five turns, powering up Water-type attacks. The rain also lowers the power of Fire-type attacks.

229.name: Rest
229.description: The user goes to sleep for two turns. This fully restores the user's HP and cures any status conditions.

229.name: Roar
229.description: The target is scared off, and a different Pokémon is dragged out. In the wild, this ends a battle against a single Pokémon.

229.name: Role Play
229.description: The user mimics the target completely, copying the target's Ability.

229.name: Scary Face
229.description: The user frightens the target with a scary face to harshly lower its Speed stat.

229.name: Sleep Talk
229.description: The user randomly uses one of the moves it knows. This move can only be used while the user is asleep.

229.name: Snatch
229.description: Steals the effects of the move the foe uses next.

229.name: Spite
229.description: The user unleashes its grudge on the move last used by the target by cutting 4 PP from it.

229.name: Substitute
229.description: The user creates a substitute for itself using some of its own HP. The substitute serves as the user's decoy.

229.name: Sunny Day
229.description: The user intensifies the sun for five turns, powering up Fire-type attacks. The sunlight also lowers the power of Water-type attacks.

229.name: Swagger
229.description: The user enrages and confuses the target. However, this also sharply boosts the target's Attack stat.

229.name: Taunt
229.description: The target is taunted into a rage that allows it to use only attack moves for three turns.

229.name: Torment
229.description: The user torments and enrages the target, making it incapable of using the same move twice in a row.

229.name: Toxic
229.description: A move that leaves the target badly poisoned. Its poison damage worsens every turn.

229.name: Will-O-Wisp

279.description: The user growls in an endearing way, making opposing Pokémon less wary. This lowers their Attack stats.

279.name: Helping Hand
279.description: The user assists an ally by boosting the power of that ally's attack.

279.description: The user copies the move last used by the target. The copied move can be used until the user of Mimic leaves the battle.

279.name: Soak

279.name: Swagger

282.description: By moving rapidly, the user makes illusory copies of itself to boost its evasiveness.

282.description: The user endures any attack with at least 1 HP. This move's chance of failing rises if used in succession.

282.description: The user creates a substitute for itself using some of its own HP. The substitute serves as the user's decoy.

282.description: The user enrages and confuses the target. However, this also sharply boosts the target's Attack stat.

306.name: Block

306.name: Captivate
306.description: If it is the opposite gender of the user, the foe is charmed into sharply lowering its Sp. Atk stat.

306.name: Confide
306.description: The user tells the target a secret, and the target loses its ability to concentrate. This lowers the target's Sp. Atk stat.

306.name: Defense Curl
306.description: The user curls up and boosts its Defense stat.

306.name: Double Team
306.description: By moving rapidly, the user makes illusory copies of itself to boost its evasiveness.

306.name: Endure
306.description: The user endures any attack with at least 1 HP. This move's chance of failing rises if used in succession.

306.name: Harden
306.description: The user stiffens all the muscles in its body to boost its Defense stat.

306.name: Hone Claws
306.description: The user sharpens its claws to boost its Attack stat and accuracy.

306.name: Iron Defense
306.description: The user hardens its body's surface like iron, sharply boosting its Defense stat.

306.name: Magnet Rise
306.description: The user levitates using electrically generated magnetism for five turns.

306.name: Metal Sound
306.description: A horrible sound like scraping metal harshly lowers the target's Sp. Def stat.

306.name: Mimic
306.description: The user copies the move last used by the target. The copied move can be used until the user of Mimic leaves the battle.

306.name: Protect
306.description: This move enables the user to protect itself from all attacks. Its chance of failing rises if it is used in succession.

306.name: Rain Dance
306.description: The user summons a heavy rain that falls for five turns, powering up Water-type attacks. The rain also lowers the power of Fire-type attacks.

306.name: Rest
306.description: The user goes to sleep for two turns. This fully restores the user's HP and cures any status conditions.

306.name: Roar
306.description: The target is scared off, and a different Pokémon is dragged out. In the wild, this ends a battle against a single Pokémon.

306.name: Rock Polish
306.description: The user polishes its body to reduce drag. This sharply boosts the user's Speed stat.

306.name: Sandstorm
306.description: A five-turn sandstorm is summoned to damage all Pokémon except Rock, Ground, and Steel types. The sandstorm also boosts the Sp. Def stats of Rock types.

306.name: Scary Face
306.description: The user frightens the target with a scary face to harshly lower its Speed stat.

306.name: Screech
306.description: An earsplitting screech harshly lowers the target's Defense stat.

306.name: Sleep Talk
306.description: The user randomly uses one of the moves it knows. This move can only be used while the user is asleep.

306.name: Spite
306.description: The user unleashes its grudge on the move last used by the target by cutting 4 PP from it.

306.name: Stealth Rock
306.description: The user lays a trap of levitating stones around the opposing team. The trap damages opposing Pokémon that switch into battle.

306.name: Substitute
306.description: The user creates a substitute for itself using some of its own HP. The substitute serves as the user's decoy.

306.name: Sunny Day
306.description: The user intensifies the sun for five turns, powering up Fire-type attacks. The sunlight also lowers the power of Water-type attacks.

306.name: Swagger
306.description: The user enrages and confuses the target. However, this also sharply boosts the target's Attack stat.

306.name: Taunt
306.description: The target is taunted into a rage that allows it to use only attack moves for three turns.

306.name: Thunder Wave
306.description: The user launches a weak jolt of electricity that paralyzes the target.

306.name: Toxic
306.description: A move that leaves the target badly poisoned. Its poison damage worsens every turn.

308.name: Giga Impact

308.name: Ice Punch

319.name: Scald
319.description: The user attacks by shooting boiling hot water at the target. This may also leave the target with a burn.

319.name: Strength
319.description: The target is slugged with a punch thrown at maximum power.

319.name: Waterfall
319.description: The user charges at the target and may make it flinch.

319.name: Zen Headbutt
319.description: The user focuses its willpower to its head and attacks the target. This may also make the target flinch.

319.name: Facade
319.description: This move's power is doubled if the user is poisoned, burned, or paralyzed.

319.name: Night Slash
319.description: The user slashes the target the instant an opportunity arises. This move has a heightened chance of landing a critical hit.

319.name: Retaliate
319.description: The user takes revenge for a fainted ally. This move's power is boosted if an ally fainted in the previous turn.

319.name: Secret Power
319.description: An attack with effects that vary by location.

319.name: Slash
319.description: The target is attacked with a slash of claws, scythes, or the like. This move has a heightened chance of landing a critical hit.

319.name: Brine
319.description: This move's power is doubled if the target's HP is at half or less.

319.name: Ice Fang
319.description: The user bites with cold-infused fangs. This may also make the target flinch or leave it frozen.

319.name: Ancient Power
319.description: The user attacks with a prehistoric power. This may also boost all the user's stats at once.

319.name: Assurance
319.description: This move's power is doubled if the target has already taken some damage in the same turn.

319.name: Avalanche
319.description: This move's power is doubled if the target has inflicted damage on the user in the same turn.

319.name: Bite
319.description: The target is bitten with viciously sharp fangs. This may also make the target flinch.

319.name: Bulldoze
319.description: The user strikes everything around it by stomping down on the ground. This lowers the Speed stats of those hit.

319.name: Flip Turn
319.description: After making its attack, the user rushes back to switch places with a party Pokémon in waiting.

319.name: Hidden Power
319.description: The power varies with the POKéMON.

319.name: Rock Tomb
319.description: The user hurls boulders at the target to inflict damage. This also lowers the target's Speed stat by cutting off its movement.

319.name: Round
319.description: The user attacks the target with a song. If others use this move, they will act immediately after the initial user, and the power of their Rounds will be boosted.

319.name: Swift
319.description: Star-shaped rays are shot at opposing Pokémon. This attack never misses.

319.name: Thief
319.description: The user attacks and steals the target's held item simultaneously. The user can't steal anything if it already holds an item.

319.name: Water Pulse
319.description: The user attacks the target with a pulsing blast of water. This may also confuse the target.

319.name: Icy Wind
319.description: The user attacks with a gust of chilled air. This also lowers opposing Pokémon's Speed stats.

319.name: Snarl
319.description: The user yells as if it's ranting about something, which lowers the Sp. Atk stats of opposing Pokémon.

319.name: Payback
319.description: The user stores power, then attacks. This move's power is doubled if the user moves after the target.

319.name: Poison Fang
319.description: The user bites the target with toxic fangs. This may also leave the target badly poisoned.

319.name: Snore
319.description: This attack can be used only if the user is asleep. The harsh noise may also make the target flinch.

319.name: Aqua Jet
319.description: The user lunges at the target to inflict damage, moving at blinding speed. This move always goes first.

319.name: Fury Cutter
319.description: The user attacks by slashing the target with scythes, claws, or the like. This attack becomes more powerful if it hits in succession.

319.name: Rock Smash
319.description: The user attacks with a punch. This may also lower the target's Defense stat.

319.name: Whirlpool
319.description: The user traps the target inside a violent, swirling whirlpool that inflicts damage for four to five turns.

319.name: Feint
319.description: This attack can hit a target using a move such as Protect or Detect. This also lifts the effects of those moves.

319.name: Scale Shot
319.description: The user attacks by shooting scales two to five times in a row. This move boosts the user's Speed stat but lowers its Defense stat.

319.name: Mud-Slap
319.description: The user hurls mud in the target's face to inflict damage and lower its accuracy.

319.name: Rage
319.description: Raises ATTACK if the user is hit.

319.name: Frustration
319.description: An attack based on lack of loyalty.

319.name: Natural Gift
319.description: The user draws power to attack by using its held Berry. The Berry determines its type and power.

319.name: Return
319.description: An attack that is based on loyalty.

319.name: Super Fang
319.description: The user chomps hard on the target with its sharp front fangs. This cuts the target's HP in half.

319.name: Agility
319.description: The user relaxes and lightens its body to move faster. This sharply boosts its Speed stat.

319.name: Attract
319.description: If the target is of the opposite gender to the user, it becomes infatuated with the user and will sometimes be unable to use its moves.

319.name: Captivate
319.description: If it is the opposite gender of the user, the foe is charmed into sharply lowering its Sp. Atk stat.

319.name: Confide
319.description: The user tells the target a secret, and the target loses its ability to concentrate. This lowers the target's Sp. Atk stat.

319.name: Double Team
319.description: By moving rapidly, the user makes illusory copies of itself to boost its evasiveness.

319.name: Endure
319.description: The user endures any attack with at least 1 HP. This move's chance of failing rises if used in succession.

319.name: Focus Energy
319.description: The user takes a deep breath and focuses so that its future attacks have a heightened chance of landing critical hits.

319.name: Hail
319.description: Summons a hailstorm that strikes every turn.

319.name: Leer
319.description: The user gives opposing Pokémon an intimidating leer that lowers their Defense stats.

319.name: Mimic
319.description: The user copies the move last used by the target. The copied move can be used until the user of Mimic leaves the battle.

319.name: Protect
319.description: This move enables the user to protect itself from all attacks. Its chance of failing rises if it is used in succession.

319.name: Rain Dance
319.description: The user summons a heavy rain that falls for five turns, powering up Water-type attacks. The rain also lowers the power of Fire-type attacks.

319.name: Rest
319.description: The user goes to sleep for two turns. This fully restores the user's HP and cures any status conditions.

319.name: Roar
319.description: The target is scared off, and a different Pokémon is dragged out. In the wild, this ends a battle against a single Pokémon.

319.name: Scary Face
319.description: The user frightens the target with a scary face to harshly lower its Speed stat.

319.name: Screech
319.description: An earsplitting screech harshly lowers the target's Defense stat.

319.name: Sleep Talk
319.description: The user randomly uses one of the moves it knows. This move can only be used while the user is asleep.

319.name: Spite
319.description: The user unleashes its grudge on the move last used by the target by cutting 4 PP from it.

319.name: Substitute
319.description: The user creates a substitute for itself using some of its own HP. The substitute serves as the user's decoy.

319.name: Swagger
319.description: The user enrages and confuses the target. However, this also sharply boosts the target's Attack stat.

319.name: Taunt
319.description: The target is taunted into a rage that allows it to use only attack moves for three turns.

319.name: Torment
319.description: The user torments and enrages the target, making it incapable of using the same move twice in a row.

319.name: Toxic
319.description: A move that leaves the target badly poisoned. Its poison damage worsens every turn.

354.name: Hex
354.description: This relentless attack does massive damage to a target affected by status conditions.

354.name: Knock Off
354.description: The user slaps down the target's held item, making it unusable for that battle. This move does more damage if the target has a held item.

354.name: Psybeam
354.description: The target is attacked with a peculiar ray. This may also confuse the target.

354.name: Feint Attack
354.description: An attack that never misses.

354.description: The power varies with the POKéMON.

354.description: The user creates a gust of repulsive wind. It may also raise all the user’s stats at once.

354.name: Pounce

354.name: Nightmare
354.description: A sleeper loses 1/4 HP every turn.

354.name: Psych Up
354.description: The user hypnotizes itself into copying any stat change made by the target.

354.name: Rain Dance

354.name: Rest
354.description: The user goes to sleep for two turns. This fully restores the user's HP and cures any status conditions.

354.name: Role Play

354.description: The user frightens the target with a scary face to harshly lower its Speed stat.

354.name: Spite

395.description: By moving rapidly, the user makes illusory copies of itself to boost its evasiveness.

395.name: Endure

395.name: Feather Dance

395.name: Growl

395.description: The user assists an ally by boosting the power of that ally's attack.

395.description: The user hardens its body's surface like iron, sharply boosting its Defense stat.

395.name: Metal Sound
395.description: A horrible sound like scraping metal harshly lowers the target's Sp. Def stat.

395.name: Mist

395.name: Protect
395.description: This move enables the user to protect itself from all attacks. Its chance of failing rises if it is used in succession.

395.name: Psych Up
395.description: The user hypnotizes itself into copying any stat change made by the target.

395.name: Quash
395.description: The user suppresses the target and makes its move go last.

395.name: Rain Dance

395.name: Substitute

395.name: Swagger

475.description: The user attacks by hurling a shadowy blob at the target. This may also lower the target’s Sp. Def stat.

475.name: Strength
475.description: The target is slugged with a punch thrown at maximum power.

475.name: Tera Blast
475.description: If the user has Terastallized, it unleashes energy of its Tera Type. This move inflicts damage using the Attack or Sp. Atk stat—whichever is higher for the user.

475.name: Throat Chop
475.description: The user attacks the target's throat, and the resultant suffering prevents the target from using sound-based moves for two turns.

475.name: X-Scissor
475.description: The user slashes at the target by crossing its scythes, claws, or the like as if they were a pair of scissors.

475.name: Zen Headbutt
475.description: The user focuses its willpower to its head and attacks the target. This may also make the target flinch.

475.name: Air Slash
475.description: The user attacks with a blade of air that slices even the sky. This may also make the target flinch.

475.name: Brick Break
475.description: The user attacks with a swift chop. This move can also break barriers, such as Light Screen and Reflect.

475.name: Drain Punch
475.description: The user attacks with an energy-draining punch. The user's HP is restored by up to half the damage taken by the target.

475.name: Fire Punch
475.description: The target is attacked with a fiery punch. This may also leave the target with a burn.

475.name: Ice Punch
475.description: The target is attacked with an icy punch. This may also leave the target frozen.

475.name: Rock Slide
475.description: Large boulders are hurled at opposing Pokémon to inflict damage. This may also make the opposing Pokémon flinch.

475.name: Signal Beam
475.description: A strange beam attack that may confuse the foe.

475.name: Thunder Punch
475.description: The target is attacked with an electrified punch. This may also leave the target with paralysis.

475.name: Aqua Cutter
475.description: The user expels pressurized water to cut at the target like a blade. This move has a heightened chance of landing a critical hit.

475.name: Facade
475.description: This move's power is doubled if the user is poisoned, burned, or paralyzed.

475.name: Headbutt
475.description: The user sticks out its head and attacks by charging straight into the target. This may also make the target flinch.

475.name: Night Slash
475.description: The user slashes the target the instant an opportunity arises. This move has a heightened chance of landing a critical hit.

475.name: Psycho Cut
475.description: The user tears at the target with blades formed by psychic power. This move has a heightened chance of landing a critical hit.

475.name: Retaliate
475.description: The user takes revenge for a fainted ally. This move's power is boosted if an ally fainted in the previous turn.

475.name: Secret Power
475.description: An attack with effects that vary by location.

475.name: Shadow Claw
475.description: The user attacks by slashing the target with a sharp claw made from shadows. This move has a heightened chance of landing a critical hit.

475.name: Slash
475.description: The target is attacked with a slash of claws, scythes, or the like. This move has a heightened chance of landing a critical hit.

475.name: Hex
475.description: This relentless attack does massive damage to a target affected by status conditions.

475.name: Knock Off
475.description: The user slaps down the target's held item, making it unusable for that battle. This move does more damage if the target has a held item.

475.name: Low Sweep
475.description: The user makes a swift attack on the target's legs, which lowers the target's Speed stat.

475.name: Psybeam
475.description: The target is attacked with a peculiar ray. This may also confuse the target.

475.name: Upper Hand
475.description: The user reacts to the target's movement and strikes with the heel of its palm, making the target flinch. This move fails if the target is not readying a priority move.

475.name: Aerial Ace
475.description: The user confounds the target with speed, then slashes. This attack never misses.

475.name: Bulldoze
475.description: The user strikes everything around it by stomping down on the ground. This lowers the Speed stats of those hit.

475.name: Hidden Power
475.description: The power varies with the POKéMON.

475.name: Magical Leaf
475.description: The user scatters curious leaves that chase the target. This attack never misses.

475.name: Revenge
475.description: An attack that gains power if injured by the foe.

475.name: Rock Tomb
475.description: The user hurls boulders at the target to inflict damage. This also lowers the target's Speed stat by cutting off its movement.

475.name: Round
475.description: The user attacks the target with a song. If others use this move, they will act immediately after the initial user, and the power of their Rounds will be boosted.

475.name: Shock Wave
475.description: The user strikes the target with a quick jolt of electricity. This attack never misses.

475.name: Swift
475.description: Star-shaped rays are shot at opposing Pokémon. This attack never misses.

475.name: Thief
475.description: The user attacks and steals the target's held item simultaneously. The user can't steal anything if it already holds an item.

475.name: Icy Wind
475.description: The user attacks with a gust of chilled air. This also lowers opposing Pokémon's Speed stats.

475.name: Charge Beam
475.description: The user attacks the target with an electric charge. The residual electricity may also boost the user's Sp. Atk stat.

475.name: Confusion
475.description: The target is hit with a weak telekinetic force to inflict damage. This may also confuse the target.

475.name: Cut
475.description: Cuts using claws, scythes, etc.

475.name: Draining Kiss
475.description: The user steals the target's HP with a kiss. The user's HP is restored by over half the damage taken by the target.

475.name: Snore
475.description: This attack can be used only if the user is asleep. The harsh noise may also make the target flinch.

475.name: Disarming Voice
475.description: Letting out a charming cry, the user does emotional damage to opposing Pokémon. This attack never misses.

475.name: Dual Chop
475.description: The user attacks its target by hitting it with brutal strikes. The target is hit twice in a row.

475.name: Echoed Voice
475.description: The user attacks the target with an echoing voice. If this move is used consecutively by any Pokémon, its power is boosted.

475.name: False Swipe
475.description: A restrained attack that prevents the target from fainting. The target is left with at least 1 HP.

475.name: Fury Cutter
475.description: The user attacks by slashing the target with scythes, claws, or the like. This attack becomes more powerful if it hits in succession.

475.name: Power-Up Punch
475.description: Striking opponents over and over makes the user’s fists harder. Hitting a target raises the Attack stat.

475.name: Rock Smash
475.description: The user attacks with a punch. This may also lower the target's Defense stat.

475.name: Vacuum Wave
475.description: The user whirls its fists to send a wave of pure vacuum at the target. This move always goes first.

475.name: Feint
475.description: This attack can hit a target using a move such as Protect or Detect. This also lifts the effects of those moves.

475.name: Mud-Slap
475.description: The user hurls mud in the target's face to inflict damage and lower its accuracy.

475.name: Stored Power
475.description: The user attacks the target with stored power. The more the user's stats are boosted, the greater the move's power.

475.name: Triple Axel
475.description: A consecutive three-kick attack that becomes more powerful with each successful hit.

475.name: Fling
475.description: The user flings its held item at the target to attack. This move's power and effects depend on the item.

475.name: Frustration
475.description: An attack based on lack of loyalty.

475.name: Grass Knot
475.description: The user snares the target with grass and trips it. The heavier the target, the greater the move's power.

475.name: Low Kick
475.description: A powerful low kick that makes the target fall over. The heavier the target, the greater the move's power.

475.name: Natural Gift
475.description: The user draws power to attack by using its held Berry. The Berry determines its type and power.

475.name: Night Shade
475.description: The user makes the target see a frightening mirage. It inflicts damage equal to the user's level.

475.name: Return
475.description: An attack that is based on loyalty.

475.name: Reversal
475.description: An all-out attack that becomes more powerful the less HP the user has.

475.name: Agility
475.description: The user relaxes and lightens its body to move faster. This sharply boosts its Speed stat.

475.name: Ally Switch
475.description: The user teleports using a strange power and switches places with one of its allies. This move's chance of failing rises if it is used in succession.

475.name: Attract
475.description: If the target is of the opposite gender to the user, it becomes infatuated with the user and will sometimes be unable to use its moves.

475.name: Bulk Up
475.description: The user tenses its muscles to bulk up its body, boosting its Attack and Defense stats.

475.name: Calm Mind
475.description: The user quietly focuses its mind and calms its spirit to boost its Sp. Atk and Sp. Def stats.

475.name: Captivate
475.description: If it is the opposite gender of the user, the foe is charmed into sharply lowering its Sp. Atk stat.

475.name: Charm
475.description: The user gazes at the target rather charmingly, making it less wary. This harshly lowers the target's Attack stat.

475.name: Coaching
475.description: The user properly coaches its ally Pokémon, boosting their Attack and Defense stats.

475.name: Confide
475.description: The user tells the target a secret, and the target loses its ability to concentrate. This lowers the target's Sp. Atk stat.

475.name: Confuse Ray
475.description: The target is exposed to a sinister ray that causes confusion.

475.name: Double Team
475.description: By moving rapidly, the user makes illusory copies of itself to boost its evasiveness.

475.name: Encore
475.description: The user compels the target to keep using the move it encored for three turns.

475.name: Endure
475.description: The user endures any attack with at least 1 HP. This move's chance of failing rises if used in succession.

475.name: Flash
475.description: Blinds the foe to reduce accuracy.

475.name: Growl
475.description: The user growls in an endearing way, making opposing Pokémon less wary. This lowers their Attack stats.

475.name: Heal Pulse
475.description: The user emits a healing pulse that restores the target's HP by up to half its max HP.

475.name: Helping Hand
475.description: The user assists an ally by boosting the power of that ally's attack.

475.name: Hypnosis
475.description: The user employs hypnotic suggestion to make the target fall asleep.

475.name: Imprison
475.description: If opposing Pokémon know any move also known by the user, they are prevented from using it.

475.name: Laser Focus
475.description: The user concentrates intensely. The attack on the next turn always results in a critical hit.

475.name: Leer
475.description: The user gives opposing Pokémon an intimidating leer that lowers their Defense stats.

475.name: Life Dew
475.description: The user scatters mysterious water around and restores the HP of itself and its ally Pokémon currently in the battle.

475.name: Light Screen
475.description: A wondrous wall of light is put up to reduce damage from special moves for five turns.

475.name: Magic Coat
475.description: Reflects special effects back to the attacker.

475.name: Magic Room
475.description: The user creates a bizarre area in which Pokémon's held items lose their effects for five turns.

475.name: Misty Terrain
475.description: This protects Pokémon on the ground from status conditions and halves damage from Dragon-type moves for five turns.

475.name: Pain Split
475.description: The user adds its HP to the target's HP, then equally splits the combined HP between the target and itself.

475.name: Protect
475.description: This move enables the user to protect itself from all attacks. Its chance of failing rises if it is used in succession.

475.name: Psych Up
475.description: The user hypnotizes itself into copying any stat change made by the target.

475.name: Psychic Terrain
475.description: This protects Pokémon on the ground from priority moves and powers up Psychic-type moves for five turns.

475.name: Quick Guard
475.description: The user protects itself and its allies from priority moves.

475.name: Rain Dance
475.description: The user summons a heavy rain that falls for five turns, powering up Water-type attacks. The rain also lowers the power of Fire-type attacks.

475.name: Recycle
475.description: The user recycles a held item that has been used in battle so it can be used again.

475.name: Reflect
475.description: A wondrous wall of light is put up to reduce damage from physical moves for five turns.

475.name: Rest
475.description: The user goes to sleep for two turns. This fully restores the user's HP and cures any status conditions.

475.name: Safeguard
475.description: The user creates a protective field that prevents status conditions for five turns.

475.name: Skill Swap
475.description: The user employs its psychic power to exchange Abilities with the target.

475.name: Sleep Talk
475.description: The user randomly uses one of the moves it knows. This move can only be used while the user is asleep.

475.name: Snatch
475.description: Steals the effects of the move the foe uses next.

475.name: Substitute
475.description: The user creates a substitute for itself using some of its own HP. The substitute serves as the user's decoy.

475.name: Sunny Day
475.description: The user intensifies the sun for five turns, powering up Fire-type attacks. The sunlight also lowers the power of Water-type attacks.

475.name: Swagger
475.description: The user enrages and confuses the target. However, this also sharply boosts the target's Attack stat.

475.name: Swords Dance
475.description: A frenetic dance to uplift the fighting spirit. This sharply boosts the user's Attack stat.

475.name: Taunt
475.description: The target is taunted into a rage that allows it to use only attack moves for three turns.

475.name: Telekinesis
475.description: The user makes the target float with its psychic power. The target is easier to hit for three turns.

475.name: Teleport
475.description: The user switches places with a party Pokémon in waiting, if any. If a wild Pokémon uses this move, it flees.

475.name: Thunder Wave
475.description: The user launches a weak jolt of electricity that paralyzes the target.

475.name: Torment
475.description: The user torments and enrages the target, making it incapable of using the same move twice in a row.

475.name: Toxic
475.description: A move that leaves the target badly poisoned. Its poison damage worsens every turn.

475.name: Trick
475.description: The user catches the target off guard and swaps the target's held item with its own.

475.name: Trick Room
475.description: The user creates a bizarre area in which slower Pokémon get to move first for five turns.

475.name: Wide Guard
475.description: The user and its allies are protected from wide-ranging attacks for one turn.

475.name: Will-O-Wisp
475.description: The user shoots a sinister flame at the target to inflict a burn.

475.name: Wonder Room
475.description: The user creates a bizarre area in which Pokémon's Defense and Sp. Def stats are swapped for five turns.

475.name: Work Up

531.description: The power varies with the POKéMON.

531.name: Incinerate
531.description: The user attacks opposing Pokémon with fire. If a Pokémon is holding a certain item, such as a Berry, the item becomes burned up and unusable.

531.name: Round
531.description: The user attacks the target with a song. If others use this move, they will act immediately after the initial user, and the power of their Rounds will be boosted.

531.name: Icy Wind
531.description: The user attacks with a gust of chilled air. This also lowers opposing Pokémon's Speed stats.

531.name: Charge Beam
531.description: The user attacks the target with an electric charge. The residual electricity may also boost the user's Sp. Atk stat.

531.name: Draining Kiss
531.description: The user steals the target's HP with a kiss. The user's HP is restored by over half the damage taken by the target.

531.name: Snore
531.description: This attack can be used only if the user is asleep. The harsh noise may also make the target flinch.

531.name: Disarming Voice
531.description: Letting out a charming cry, the user does emotional damage to opposing Pokémon. This attack never misses.

531.name: Echoed Voice
531.description: The user attacks the target with an echoing voice. If this move is used consecutively by any Pokémon, its power is boosted.

531.name: Pound
531.description: The target is physically pounded with a long tail, a foreleg, or the like.

531.name: Power-Up Punch
531.description: Striking opponents over and over makes the user’s fists harder. Hitting a target raises the Attack stat.

531.name: Double Slap
531.description: Repeatedly slaps 2-5 times.

531.name: Fling
531.description: The user flings its held item at the target to attack. This move's power and effects depend on the item.

531.name: Frustration
531.description: An attack based on lack of loyalty.

531.name: Grass Knot
531.description: The user snares the target with grass and trips it. The heavier the target, the greater the move's power.

531.name: Low Kick
531.description: A powerful low kick that makes the target fall over. The heavier the target, the greater the move's power.

531.name: Return
531.description: An attack that is based on loyalty.

531.name: After You
531.description: The user helps the target and makes it use its move right after the user.

531.name: Ally Switch
531.description: The user teleports using a strange power and switches places with one of its allies. This move's chance of failing rises if it is used in succession.

531.name: Amnesia
531.description: The user temporarily empties its mind to forget its concerns. This sharply boosts the user's Sp. Def stat.

531.name: Attract
531.description: If the target is of the opposite gender to the user, it becomes infatuated with the user and will sometimes be unable to use its moves.

531.name: Baby-Doll Eyes
531.description: The user stares at the target with its baby-doll eyes, which lowers the target's Attack stat. This move always goes first.

531.name: Bestow
531.description: The user passes its held item to the target when the target isn’t holding an item.

531.name: Calm Mind
531.description: The user quietly focuses its mind and calms its spirit to boost its Sp. Atk and Sp. Def stats.

531.name: Confide
531.description: The user tells the target a secret, and the target loses its ability to concentrate. This lowers the target's Sp. Atk stat.

531.name: Double Team
531.description: By moving rapidly, the user makes illusory copies of itself to boost its evasiveness.

531.name: Encore
531.description: The user compels the target to keep using the move it encored for three turns.

531.name: Endure
531.description: The user endures any attack with at least 1 HP. This move's chance of failing rises if used in succession.

531.name: Entrainment
531.description: The user dances with an odd rhythm that compels the target to mimic it, making the target's Ability the same as the user's.

531.name: Flash
531.description: Blinds the foe to reduce accuracy.

531.name: Gravity
531.description: Enables Flying types or Pokémon with the Levitate Ability to be hit by Ground-type moves for five turns. Moves that involve flying can't be used.

531.name: Growl
531.description: The user growls in an endearing way, making opposing Pokémon less wary. This lowers their Attack stats.

531.name: Heal Bell
531.description: The user makes a soothing bell chime to cure the status conditions of all its fellow party Pokémon and allies.

531.name: Heal Pulse
531.description: The user emits a healing pulse that restores the target's HP by up to half its max HP.

531.name: Healing Wish
531.description: The user faints. In return, the Pokémon taking its place will have its HP restored and status conditions cured.

531.name: Helping Hand
531.description: The user assists an ally by boosting the power of that ally's attack.

531.name: Laser Focus
531.description: The user concentrates intensely. The attack on the next turn always results in a critical hit.

531.name: Life Dew
531.description: The user scatters mysterious water around and restores the HP of itself and its ally Pokémon currently in the battle.

531.name: Light Screen
531.description: A wondrous wall of light is put up to reduce damage from special moves for five turns.

531.name: Lucky Chant
531.description: The user chants an incantation toward the sky, preventing the foe from landing critical hits.

531.name: Magic Coat
531.description: Reflects special effects back to the attacker.

531.name: Misty Terrain
531.description: This protects Pokémon on the ground from status conditions and halves damage from Dragon-type moves for five turns.

531.name: Pain Split
531.description: The user adds its HP to the target's HP, then equally splits the combined HP between the target and itself.

531.name: Play Nice
531.description: The user and the target become friends, and the target loses its will to fight. This lowers the target's Attack stat.

531.name: Protect
531.description: This move enables the user to protect itself from all attacks. Its chance of failing rises if it is used in succession.

531.name: Psych Up
531.description: The user hypnotizes itself into copying any stat change made by the target.

531.name: Rain Dance
531.description: The user summons a heavy rain that falls for five turns, powering up Water-type attacks. The rain also lowers the power of Fire-type attacks.

531.name: Reflect
531.description: A wondrous wall of light is put up to reduce damage from physical moves for five turns.

531.name: Refresh
531.description: Heals poisoning, paralysis, or a burn.

531.name: Rest
531.description: The user goes to sleep for two turns. This fully restores the user's HP and cures any status conditions.

531.name: Role Play
531.description: The user mimics the target completely, copying the target's Ability.

531.name: Safeguard
531.description: The user creates a protective field that prevents status conditions for five turns.

531.name: Simple Beam
531.description: The user emits a mysterious psychic wave that changes the target's Ability to Simple.

531.name: Skill Swap
531.description: The user employs its psychic power to exchange Abilities with the target.

531.name: Sleep Talk
531.description: The user randomly uses one of the moves it knows. This move can only be used while the user is asleep.

531.name: Snatch
531.description: Steals the effects of the move the foe uses next.

531.name: Substitute
531.description: The user creates a substitute for itself using some of its own HP. The substitute serves as the user's decoy.

531.name: Sunny Day
531.description: The user intensifies the sun for five turns, powering up Fire-type attacks. The sunlight also lowers the power of Water-type attacks.

531.name: Swagger
531.description: The user enrages and confuses the target. However, this also sharply boosts the target's Attack stat.

531.name: Sweet Kiss
531.description: The user kisses the target with a sweet, angelic cuteness that causes confusion.

531.name: Telekinesis
531.description: The user makes the target float with its psychic power. The target is easier to hit for three turns.

531.name: Thunder Wave
531.description: The user launches a weak jolt of electricity that paralyzes the target.

531.name: Toxic
531.description: A move that leaves the target badly poisoned. Its poison damage worsens every turn.

531.name: Trick Room
531.description: The user creates a bizarre area in which slower Pokémon get to move first for five turns.

531.name: Wish

531.name: Work Up
531.description: The user is roused, and its Attack and Sp. Atk stats are boosted.

531.name: Yawn

623.description: The user slaps down the target's held item, making it unusable for that battle. This move does more damage if the target has a held item.

623.name: Low Sweep
623.description: The user makes a swift attack on the target's legs, which lowers the target's Speed stat.

623.name: Bulldoze
623.description: The user strikes everything around it by stomping down on the ground. This lowers the Speed stats of those hit.

623.name: Hidden Power
623.description: The power varies with the POKéMON.

623.name: Rock Tomb
623.description: The user hurls boulders at the target to inflict damage. This also lowers the target's Speed stat by cutting off its movement.

623.name: Round
623.description: The user attacks the target with a song. If others use this move, they will act immediately after the initial user, and the power of their Rounds will be boosted.

623.name: Shadow Punch
623.description: The user throws a punch from the shadows. This attack never misses.

623.name: Shock Wave
623.description: The user strikes the target with a quick jolt of electricity. This attack never misses.

623.name: Thief
623.description: The user attacks and steals the target's held item simultaneously. The user can't steal anything if it already holds an item.

623.name: Icy Wind
623.description: The user attacks with a gust of chilled air. This also lowers opposing Pokémon's Speed stats.

623.name: Charge Beam
623.description: The user attacks the target with an electric charge. The residual electricity may also boost the user's Sp. Atk stat.

623.name: Smack Down
623.description: The user throws a stone or similar projectile to attack the target. If the target is in the air, it will be knocked down to the ground.

623.name: Snore
623.description: This attack can be used only if the user is asleep. The harsh noise may also make the target flinch.

623.name: Pound
623.description: The target is physically pounded with a long tail, a foreleg, or the like.

623.name: Power-Up Punch
623.description: Striking opponents over and over makes the user’s fists harder. Hitting a target raises the Attack stat.

623.name: Rock Smash
623.description: The user attacks with a punch. This may also lower the target's Defense stat.

623.name: Astonish
623.description: The user attacks the target by crying out in a startling fashion. This may also make the target flinch.

623.name: Rollout
623.description: The user continually rolls into the target over five turns. This attack becomes more powerful each time it hits.

623.name: Mud-Slap
623.description: The user hurls mud in the target's face to inflict damage and lower its accuracy.

623.name: Fling
623.description: The user flings its held item at the target to attack. This move's power and effects depend on the item.

623.name: Frustration
623.description: An attack based on lack of loyalty.

623.name: Grass Knot
623.description: The user snares the target with grass and trips it. The heavier the target, the greater the move's power.

623.name: Gyro Ball
623.description: The user tackles the target with a high-speed spin. The slower the user is than the target, the greater the move's power.

623.name: Hard Press
623.description: The target is crushed with an arm, a claw, or the like to inflict damage. The more HP the target has left, the greater the move's power.

623.name: Heat Crash
623.description: The user slams into the target with its flame-covered body. The more the user outweighs the target, the greater the move's power.

623.name: Heavy Slam
623.description: The user slams into the target with its heavy body. The more the user outweighs the target, the greater the move's power.

623.name: Low Kick
623.description: A powerful low kick that makes the target fall over. The heavier the target, the greater the move's power.

623.name: Magnitude
623.description: A ground attack with random power.

623.name: Night Shade
623.description: The user makes the target see a frightening mirage. It inflicts damage equal to the user's level.

623.name: Return
623.description: An attack that is based on loyalty.

623.name: Ally Switch
623.description: The user teleports using a strange power and switches places with one of its allies. This move's chance of failing rises if it is used in succession.

623.name: Block
623.description: The user blocks the target's way with arms spread wide to prevent the target from fleeing.

623.name: Confide
623.description: The user tells the target a secret, and the target loses its ability to concentrate. This lowers the target's Sp. Atk stat.

623.name: Confuse Ray
623.description: The target is exposed to a sinister ray that causes confusion.

623.name: Curse
623.description: A move that has different effects depending on whether the user is a Ghost type or not.

623.name: Defense Curl
623.description: The user curls up and boosts its Defense stat.

623.name: Double Team
623.description: By moving rapidly, the user makes illusory copies of itself to boost its evasiveness.

623.name: Endure
623.description: The user endures any attack with at least 1 HP. This move's chance of failing rises if used in succession.

623.name: Flash
623.description: Blinds the foe to reduce accuracy.

623.name: Gravity
623.description: Enables Flying types or Pokémon with the Levitate Ability to be hit by Ground-type moves for five turns. Moves that involve flying can't be used.

623.name: Helping Hand
623.description: The user assists an ally by boosting the power of that ally's attack.

623.name: Imprison
623.description: If opposing Pokémon know any move also known by the user, they are prevented from using it.

623.name: Iron Defense
623.description: The user hardens its body's surface like iron, sharply boosting its Defense stat.

623.name: Magic Coat
623.description: Reflects special effects back to the attacker.

623.name: Protect
623.description: This move enables the user to protect itself from all attacks. Its chance of failing rises if it is used in succession.

623.name: Psych Up
623.description: The user hypnotizes itself into copying any stat change made by the target.

623.name: Rain Dance
623.description: The user summons a heavy rain that falls for five turns, powering up Water-type attacks. The rain also lowers the power of Fire-type attacks.

623.name: Reflect
623.description: A wondrous wall of light is put up to reduce damage from physical moves for five turns.

623.name: Rest
623.description: The user goes to sleep for two turns. This fully restores the user's HP and cures any status conditions.

623.name: Rock Polish
623.description: The user polishes its body to reduce drag. This sharply boosts the user's Speed stat.

623.name: Safeguard
623.description: The user creates a protective field that prevents status conditions for five turns.

623.name: Sandstorm
623.description: A five-turn sandstorm is summoned to damage all Pokémon except Rock, Ground, and Steel types. The sandstorm also boosts the Sp. Def stats of Rock types.

623.name: Sleep Talk
623.description: The user randomly uses one of the moves it knows. This move can only be used while the user is asleep.

623.name: Stealth Rock
623.description: The user lays a trap of levitating stones around the opposing team. The trap damages opposing Pokémon that switch into battle.

623.name: Substitute
623.description: The user creates a substitute for itself using some of its own HP. The substitute serves as the user's decoy.

623.name: Sunny Day
623.description: The user intensifies the sun for five turns, powering up Fire-type attacks. The sunlight also lowers the power of Water-type attacks.

623.name: Swagger
623.description: The user enrages and confuses the target. However, this also sharply boosts the target's Attack stat.

623.name: Telekinesis
623.description: The user makes the target float with its psychic power. The target is easier to hit for three turns.

623.name: Toxic
623.description: A move that leaves the target badly poisoned. Its poison damage worsens every turn.

623.name: Trick
623.description: The user catches the target off guard and swaps the target's held item with its own.
